38: **Spain** and **Saudi Arabia** meet in **Group H** with both sides level on a single point among all four teams, positions entirely unseparated — meaning neither can afford a second straight draw. **Spain** bring the pressure of expectation and superior individual quality, but **Saudi Arabia** have already shown resilience this stage and will look to frustrate and hit on the counter in what could be a genuinely tight affair.
39: **Belgium** and **IR Iran** are both level on one point in a completely deadlocked **Group G**, where all four sides share identical records and no position has yet been separated. The Red Devils have the attacking firepower to break the stalemate, but **IR Iran**'s disciplined defensive block made life uncomfortable in their opener, and they'll back themselves to nick something here if **Belgium** grow impatient.
37: **Uruguay** and **Cabo Verde** both sit level on one point in **Group H** alongside the group's other two sides, with positions still entirely unresolved heading into this 3:00 PM PT showdown. **Uruguay**'s battle-hardened South American pedigree makes them slight favourites, but **Cabo Verde** have proven they are no pushovers and a point here could still be enough to keep their knockout hopes very much alive.
40: **New Zealand** and **Egypt** close out a pivotal day for **Group G** at 6:00 PM PT, both sitting level on a single point in a group where all four teams remain completely inseparable. With **Belgium** and **IR Iran** having already played, one or both of these sides could know exactly what they need — and the All Whites and the Pharaohs will leave everything on the pitch to avoid falling off the pace.
34: **Ecuador** and **Curaçao** are both yet to earn a point in **Group E**, though **Ecuador** have played one game to **Curaçao**'s one as well — both sitting level on zero points with positions not yet separated. La Tri's more organised defensive structure should prove decisive, but with their knockout hopes already hanging by a thread, they cannot afford to treat this as a formality against a Curaçao side eager to make history.
36: **Tunisia** are bottom of **Group F** on zero points after one game, already facing serious elimination pressure, while **Japan** carry one point from their opener — level with one other team in the group with their position not yet separated. The Samurai Blue's quick, incisive passing game will test **Tunisia**'s defensive resolve, and the Carthage Eagles know that anything less than three points here will almost certainly end their tournament before it truly begins.
